entries.title: World's Top Universities (Ivy League & Elite)
entries.content: For elite institutions, requirements are very high: MIT (#1, GPA 3.9, IELTS 7.3, Tuition ~$28k), Harvard (#2, GPA 3.9, IELTS 6.8, Tuition ~$25k), Stanford (#3, GPA 3.9, IELTS 7.2, Tuition ~$42k). In the UK, Oxford (#4) and Cambridge (#5) require GPA 3.8 and IELTS 7.4-7.5.

entries.title: University Options and Requirements in Canada
entries.content: Canada has options for all levels: University of Toronto (#18) is top-tier (GPA 3.6, IELTS 7.4, Tuition ~$49k). Mid-range schools include Admin Test University (GPA 3.2, IELTS 6.5, $32k) and Stewartland University (#253, GPA 3.1, IELTS 6.2, $11.5k). Low GPA options like Sanchezfort accept 2.6 GPA.

entries.title: Study in Europe: Germany, France, Switzerland, and Sweden
entries.content: Europe offers affordable quality education. ETH Zurich (#11, GPA 3.7) is elite. France's Hurstfurt University is very cheap ($8,452, GPA 2.8). In Germany, Rodneymouth (#210) accepts 2.6 GPA and Kaylamouth (#378) accepts 3.0 GPA. Sweden's Lake Larry accepts 2.5 GPA.

entries.title: University Options in Singapore and Australia
entries.content: NUS Singapore (#19) requires GPA 3.6 and IELTS 6.7. University of Melbourne (#33) in Australia is premium (Tuition ~$55k, GPA 3.5). For lower requirements in Australia, Lake Roberto (#509) accepts GPA 2.9 and IELTS 6.0.

entries.title: GIC, Tuition, and Financial Proof for Visa
entries.content: For Canada's SDS stream, a GIC of $20,635 CAD (2024/25 rate) is mandatory. Students must also show proof of paying the first year's tuition. Living costs average $1,500-$2,200 CAD per month depending on the city (Toronto/Vancouver being highest).

entries.title: Complete Document Checklist for Applications
entries.content: Essential documents: 1. Valid Passport 2. Attested Transcripts (HEC/IBCC) 3. Statement of Purpose (SOP) 4. Two Academic LORs 5. English Test Scores (IELTS/PTE/Duolingo) 6. CV 7. Proof of Finances (GIC/Bank Statement).

entries.title: Part-time Jobs and Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP)
entries.content: Students can work 20 hours/week during studies and 40 hours during breaks. Average pay is $15-$17/hr. After graduation, 2+ year programs qualify for a 3-year PGWP, which is a primary pathway to Permanent Residency (PR) in Canada.

entries.title: Timeline, Intakes, and Admission Strategy
entries.content: Start 10 months early. Fall (Sept) is the main intake (deadlines Jan-March). Winter (Jan) deadlines are June-Aug. For low GPA (2.5-2.8), target Germany (Rodneymouth), France (Hurstfurt), or Canada (Sanchezfort). For IELTS 6.0, target South Patrickmouth (UK) or Lake Roberto (Australia).

entries.title: Master's Degree vs. Post-Graduate (PG) Diploma
entries.content: Master's degrees provide higher PR points and are research-focused but expensive. PG Diplomas are 1-2 years, industry-focused, cheaper, and excellent for students wanting to enter the job market quickly with PGWP eligibility.

entries.title: The Study Advisor Partnered Institutions
entries.content: Admin Test University in Toronto, Canada, is a partnered institution. Partnered status may include application fee waivers ($120) and prioritized processing for our students.
